claude.note: Headless Claude Code. Skills auto-load from .claude/skills; project CLAUDE.md supplies the operating notes. Tighten --permission-mode to acceptEdits if full autonomy is not wanted.

codex.note: Headless Codex CLI (>=0.144, ChatGPT auth). The executable resolves from PATH so the same configuration works on development machines and replaceable cloud runners. Project skills resolve through .agents/skills to the same git-controlled production + trainer method used by Claude. --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox is the unattended parity of claude's bypassPermissions; run only inside the dedicated forecast workspace.

prompts.live_forecast: Use the technology-company-profit-forecasting skill for a complete live forecast of {entity} ({security}). The value {as_of} is snapshot metadata and workspace identity only; it is not a source cutoff. Use all current evidence through bundle freeze. Create {workspace} with scripts/scaffold_delivery.py, then follow the production SOP and shared contracts. Build the smallest causal operating and financial model that closes every material path to revenue, operating profit and GAAP attributable net income; preserve raw evidence, formulas, direct lineage, rival explanations and falsification monitors. Do not replace investment judgment with scores, manual weights or artifact-count quotas. Publish only after strict validation passes, or mark the result not-decision-ready with the unresolved material gates. Populate canonical output fields and the dashboard valuation compatibility view from the same snapshot. Record method version and hashes honestly; never fabricate sources. Obey AGENTS.md. {extra}
prompts.training_case: Use the technology-company-forecasting-trainer skill for one sealed historical case: entity={entity} ({security}), as_of={as_of}, round_id={round_id}, case_role={case_role}, workspace={workspace}. Here as_of is the historical evidence cutoff. Scaffold with scripts/scaffold_training_run.py, research only inside that sandbox, build the same causal operating and financial contract as production, and seal the forecast before retrieving target actuals. After the seal, retrieve actuals and diagnose errors by evidence meaning, causal edge, equation, accounting link, cycle/persistence judgment, valuation or uncertainty calibration. Metrics are diagnostics, not a promotion objective. Keep round.json honest, check control.json before work, stop cleanly on pause, and do not edit the skill system in a single-case run. Never encode the known answer, fabricate a source or replace causal reasoning with a score. {extra}
prompts.training_round: Use the technology-company-forecasting-trainer skill and references/historical-training-loop.md for one case-selected round. round_id={round_id}; development Group A={group_a}; untouched Group B={group_b}; workspaces are under {runs_root}/{round_id}/. Record the base method commit and seal every historical forecast before retrieving actuals. Change only canonical, general evidence, causal, operating, accounting or uncertainty rules that address a named recurring failure; never encode company answers or sector weights. Test each claimed improvement on untouched independent validation cases and have independent agents judge evidence meaning, causal logic, accounting closure and generalization. Metrics are diagnostic vectors: no aggregate score, fixed artifact quota or case count decides promotion. Use an optional cross-fold diagnostic only when it can distinguish competing failure explanations; it is not mandatory. Promote only the supported scope, with a stopping rule and explicit unresolved rivals. For promotion run scripts/build_skill_system.py --promote --self-test with the required promotion evidence, then the full orthogonal regression suite before commit and push. If evidence is inconsistent, limit or abandon the change and record why. Check control.json before each case and phase; never record a bare pass. {extra}
prompts.plan_round: Use the technology-company-forecasting-trainer skill. Plan only; do not run a forecast. Read the curriculum and existing rounds under {runs_root}. Build a case-selected round using the smallest set that can discriminate the named failure hypothesis: at least one development case and at least one untouched independent validation case. Select for distinct economic mechanisms, lifecycle/cycle states and evidence conditions, not a fixed count or aggregate score; do not reuse a case across groups. State the claim boundary, rival explanations and stopping rule. Write {runs_root}/<round-id>/round.json (use {round_id} when supplied, otherwise the next free round id) with status planned, base_method_commit, normalized group_a/group_b entries (entity, security, as_of, case_id, role), and notes explaining why each case is necessary. Preserve unrelated round and case extension fields when revising an existing plan. Then print the plan as JSON. {extra}
prompts.suggest_watch: You are the stock-research assistant for the technology-company forecasting dashboard. This is a WATCHLIST SUGGESTION task only: do not run a forecast, use the trainer, or modify anything except {suggestions_path}. Current watchlist: {watchlist}. Direction hint: {hint}. Recommend 5-8 US-listed technology companies not already listed. Diversify by reusable economic primitives and industry states—unit/price/mix, capacity/utilization/yield, order-to-revenue, usage/unit cost, recurring retention, working-capital cycle, technology qualification and reinvestment economics—rather than by a fixed sector scorecard. Prefer companies whose profit and cash drivers are disclosed well enough to model and monitor. Verify the primary US ticker. Write exactly one JSON object with generated_at, hint and suggestions; each suggestion has official entity, security and one Chinese sentence <=40 chars explaining the modelable profit driver, with no price target.

quotes.note: Set proxy to e.g. http://127.0.0.1:10808 if Yahoo is unreachable directly.
